title: Ukrainian Clam Bisque
description: A rich and creamy bisque inspired by Ukrainian cuisine, featuring succulent clams as the main ingredient. This dish is perfect for a chilly evening.

ingredients.name: clams
ingredients.notes: Fresh or frozen clams work well for this recipe.

ingredients.name: butter
ingredients.notes: Unsalted butter is recommended for this recipe.

ingredients.name: onion
ingredients.notes: Chop the onion before sautéing it.

ingredients.name: garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic before adding it to the pot.

ingredients.name: white wine
ingredients.notes: Dry white wine is best for this recipe.

ingredients.name: fish stock
ingredients.notes: Homemade or store-bought fish stock works well.

ingredients.name: heavy cream
ingredients.notes: Heavy cream adds richness to the bisque.

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: Season the bisque with salt to taste.

ingredients.name: paprika
ingredients.notes: Smoked or sweet paprika works well in this recipe.

instructions: Heat the butter in a large pot over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and sauté for another minute.
Add the clams, white wine, fish stock, and paprika to the pot. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until the clams are cooked through.
Use an immersion blender to puree the bisque until smooth.
Stir in the heavy cream and season the bisque with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the bisque hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or dill if desired.
